TextENGNew: "...My apologies for being late.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1248.There was an unusually large number of patients today."
"Thank you for your hard work, Lieutenant Colonel Irie!"
changeReason: Original says 'Thank you for coming' but JPN お疲れ様です means 'Thank you for your hard work'; new translation is more accurate.

TextENGNew: Tomitake, who had been lounging comfortably, stood up and straightened his posture the moment he saw Irie arrive.
changeReason: Original adds 'saluted' (not in JPN); new translation correctly captures 'straightened his posture' (背筋を伸ばした) and adds context about lounging.

TextENGNew: "Oh, please, stop with that Lieutenant Colonel business...!@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1250. I am a doctor. I simply cannot get used to these military ranks.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1251.At the very least, call me Director..."
"Understood, Director Irie.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1021. Ahahahaha...!"
By now, this had become something of a running joke, since Tomitake knew full well that Irie disliked being addressed by his military rank.
Tomitake and the others shared an easygoing laugh.
Takano, however, looked thoroughly fed up.
She could not stand hearing the same joke repeated over and over, and had been meaning to tell Tomitake to stop with the Lieutenant Colonel bit if the opportunity arose.
...But she kept putting it off, knowing that if she said anything, it would hurt his feelings in ways he would never show.
Also, perhaps because of the way Tomitake pronounced it, "nisa" always sounded like "Risa" to her.
Once she heard it that way, every time he said it, she could only hear "Irie Risa," "Irie Risa."
When Takano had shared this privately with Irie, he found it absolutely hilarious...
Today was merely a greeting; the actual meeting was scheduled for tomorrow.
Tomitake had already arrived today, but tomorrow, several inspectors from "Tokyo" would join them to receive a briefing on the organization's research progress.
changeReason: New translation puts 'Tokyo' in quotes, reflecting the JPN 『東京』 which signals a code name for a shadowy organization rather than the literal city, changing the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: "By the way, it seems the whole village is in an uproar over the dam project."

"You can say that again!@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1253. The village is in absolute chaos.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1254.I attended the local government briefing as an official, but... it turned into quite the spectacle, filled with shouting and profanity."
After the Hinamizawa Dam Basic Plan was announced, the village had erupted into pandemonium, as was to be expected.
The government retracted its concession proposals fairly early on.
They made their aggressive stance unmistakably clear.
Their reasoning seemed to be that showing any willingness to negotiate would only embolden the opposition.
However, that calculation backfired severely.

In Hinamizawa, where communal solidarity rooted in the land ran historically deep, such heavy-handedness only provoked greater unity.@k Both the government and the village had dug in, and the conflict had devolved into a quagmire where neither side could yield.
"Hee hee hee.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1213. The people of Hinamizawa do have quite the fierce temperament.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1214.I wonder if it's because demon blood runs through their veins."
"This is no laughing matter.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1256. Try sitting through that meeting until the middle of the night and see how you like it."
"You have my sympathies.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1024. Ha ha ha ha...!"
"No, really, it's not funny, Tomitake-san.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1258. What is the status of the dam project?@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1259. Is this village truly going to be sunk beneath a reservoir?"

"Tokyo is applying pressure on that front as well.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1026. On the surface, things may look tumultuous, but behind the scenes, the project's foundation should be crumbling considerably."
"...I hope that pressure takes effect soon.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1261. The village has been in a frenzy from dawn to dusk, and I fear the villagers' spirits are growing increasingly agitated."
changeReason: Old reverses 'dawn to dusk' as 'dusk to dawn.' The Japanese says 'from morning to evening.' A factual error but the broader meaning (constant activity) is preserved.

TextENGNew: "Sometimes the elderly patients corner me during their appointments and give me an earful about their anti-dam arguments."
changeReason: New translation specifies 'elderly patients' matching JPN お年寄り, where original just says 'patients,' omitting the age detail.

TextENGNew: "Hee hee.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1216. Why don't you just refuse to indulge them? You're too kind, Director."

"I can't very well do that.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1264. Before being the director of the research institute, I am the director of the clinic.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1265.Listening to their concerns is part of their care."
"That's a very admirable attitude.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1028. I have nothing but respect for you."
"I have the benefit of knowing that the people in "Tokyo" will eventually apply enough pressure to get the dam plan cancelled."
changeReason: New translation correctly places 'Tokyo' in quotes to match JPN 『東京』 (code name for organization), and removes 'but the villagers don't' which belongs to the next line.

TextENGNew: "But for the villagers, who face the very real threat of being driven from the land their families have lived on for generations, it truly is an enormous crisis."

"Even if they're told to leave, they have neither the money nor the youth to relocate.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1216b. Perhaps some of them had hoped to negotiate higher compensation, but then the government abruptly changed its stance and now it's all-out war."
changeReason: New translation adds 'all-out war' for JPN 全面衝突 (head-on collision), which the original omitted, changing the severity of the described conflict.

TextENGNew: "...At this point, a peaceful resolution may well be impossible."

"There were once a few people willing to accept the compensation and leave.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1271. But the Sonozaki family, one of the Three Great Families, has been leading the villagers with an unyielding stance.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1272.They appear to be actively fanning the confrontation with the government."
changeReason: New translation adds 'one of the Three Great Families' (御三家) and uses 'fanning the confrontation' (closer to JPN 煽っている) vs original's 'leading the fight,' conveying more deliberate provocation.

TextENGNew: "Propaganda vans blare through the streets constantly, and flyers, leaflets, and newsletters keep making the rounds alongside the community bulletin."
"It's a serious matter for the people living here.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1030. I can certainly understand their feelings."
"...In that sense, while our motives may differ somewhat, for the sake of all these people, I do hope the negotiations move forward quickly and the dam plan is withdrawn."
changeReason: New translation includes 'while our motives may differ somewhat' (思惑は多少違うかもしれませんが), hinting at Irie's secret agenda, which the original omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: "Once it's withdrawn, the village should quickly return to its former peaceful self."
"Of course, I understand.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1032. Negotiations are moving forward in Tokyo, so please leave it to us for a little while longer."
"...I've heard a rumor from Tokyo that you're having difficulty finding the right connections to effectively pressure the Ministry of Construction. Is that true?"
changeReason: Original says 'Minister of Construction' (a person) but JPN 建設省 means 'Ministry of Construction' (an institution); new translation corrects this.

TextENGNew: "Well... ha ha ha...@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1034. I'm not privy to the details, but I have heard murmurs that the behind-the-scenes negotiations aren't going entirely smoothly."
Takano's backers were an immensely powerful faction, but that did not mean they had the entire country under their thumb.
There were areas where their influence was strong, and others where it was not.
...Having the right connections to pressure the Ministry of Construction was evidently not among their strengths.
changeReason: Same correction as prior entry: original says 'Minister of Construction' (person) but JPN 建設省 means 'Ministry of Construction' (institution).

TextENGNew: "All joking aside,@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1220. we truly won't find ourselves being told to vacate because a dam is going up... will we?"

"O-Of course not.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1036. On that point, there is absolutely no cause for concern.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1037.It's simply taking time. So please, leave that to us and focus on advancing the research."
"In the event that a peaceful resolution proves impossible, there is consensus that drastic measures may be taken as a last resort."
"Drastic measures...? What do you mean by that, Tomitake-san?"
"That falls within our jurisdiction.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1040. You needn't concern yourself with it, Director Irie.@k@vS20/08/VTK1_tomitake1041.Please, leave it in our hands."
"......Is that so.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1278. ...Very well.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1279.Thank you for your efforts."

"It will be fine, Director Irie.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1222. The dam plan will absolutely be withdrawn.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1223. ...After all, it's already been decided.@k@vS20/09/VTK1_takano1224. ...Hee hee."
As Takano let out her quiet laugh, Tomitake followed suit with a thin smile of his own.
...Irie alone could not share in their amusement, and felt a faint sense of exclusion...
......Later, when Irie was asked to examine a young boy he had never seen in the village before, he would come to understand exactly what those "drastic measures" meant.
